title: Violation Is the Connective Tissue in This Family Portrait
description:
The drama of The Complex, Karan Mahajan’s new novel, is set off by a sexual assault. Gita, who has recently married into the esteemed Chopra family, travels back to Delhi from the United States to visit family and attend a wedding of one of her husband’s relatives. There, she runs into her husband’s uncle, Laxman, […]

The post Violation Is the Connective Tissue in This Family Port
hook: A marriage implodes when a woman returns to Delhi and confronts her husband's family secrets—trauma as the thing that binds them all together.

title: Gout
description: "What the disease taught me about language, inheritance and pain."
hook: A disease becomes a gateway to understanding language, what you inherit, and how pain rewrites your body's dictionary.

title: “Fungi Dictated the Structure of the Movie”: Otilia Portillo Padua on Daughters of the Forest
description: For generations, Indigenous women in Mexico have understood the vast power of mushrooms—medicinal, culinary, spiritual, toxic. Their knowledge has been calibrated and pa
hook: A filmmaker let fungi dictate her film's structure—what happens when you stop controlling and start listening to the material itself.

title: A Cave of Wonders: Alexander Hacke of Einstürzende Neubauten on the Record Shop that Made Him
description:
The Neubaten guitarist (later bassist) and collaborator with Phew, The Tiger Lillies and many more opens up about days wiled away in a seminal Berlin record shop, in an exclusive extract from his new book, Blast: Distorted Memories

Alexander Hacke, riding the s-bahn home after a hard day's shopping at Zensor

The ‘Zensor’ was located in Schö
hook: The guitarist who shaped industrial music traces it all back to one Berlin record shop—fandom as a formative geography.

title: Beverly Buchanan’s Architecture of Care
description: Her practice was one of embodied noticing — exploring, feeling out, or reaching longingly for a Southern architectural vernacular.
hook: Beverly Buchanan's buildings aren't monuments—they're acts of care, a way of touching Southern architecture with reverence and longing.

title: Sagrado Corazón
description: “The most likely scenario is that the men neither slid in nor slithered out. Men who dream up bombs you can strap around women’s necks are not creatures of nuance and subtlety.”
hook: Men who engineer terror don't do subtlety or nuance—this story knows exactly what that means and what it costs.

title: “Billy Could Have Been My Own Brother”: Rachel Mason on Her SXSW Doc My Brother’s Killer
description: Rachel Mason’s gripping true crime doc My Brother’s Killer is, first and foremost, a love letter.
hook: A filmmaker confronts the man who killed her brother—what happens when you make that reckoning public and unflinching.
